summary: Make WTS requirement confirmation create a durable workflow-start intent and outbox item.
rationale: Task 0B intentionally crosses the conversation-agent control plane and Workbench BFF route boundary so recruiter confirmation can atomically queue workflow start through WorkflowStartIntent instead of relying on a separate frontend workflow/start call.
line_count_rationale: Task 0B review blocker requires runtime approved-requirement save to recover same-key uniqueness conflicts without leaking sqlite3.IntegrityError. The existing store is already above the repository redline; this patch adds only the idempotency conflict branch needed for the approved-revision recovery contract.
verification: uv run pytest tests/test_conversation_agent_service.py tests/test_agent_final_summary.py tests/test_runtime_control_store.py tests/test_runtime_control_requirements.py tests/test_workflow_runtime_control_integration.py -q

uv run ruff check src/seektalent_conversation_agent src/seektalent_runtime_control src/seektalent_ui tests/test_wts_control_plane.py tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py tests/test_conversation_agent_service.py tests/test_runtime_control_requirements.py
deletion_targets: normal recruiter confirm flow depending on a separate frontend workflow/start call
runtime start parameters recovered from frontend payloads instead of canonical job request revisions
duplicate workflow starts for repeated confirmation attempts
risks: The confirm path must not create duplicate workflow-start intents or outbox rows under retries.
The pre-approval confirm request hash must catch changed expected/base and job-request body identity during crash recovery.
The outbox worker must pass the approved requirement object to the runtime executor, not an approved revision ID string.
Source selection must remain behind an explicit resolver so recruiter source kinds are validated before runtime enqueue.
